content:
Character: Selene Papadimitriou
Perception: Selene comes in every morning at 7:30, orders black coffee, sits at the same corner table, opens her laptop. She's attractive in an angular, unconventional way — strong features, watchful eyes, hands always busy. She's highly capable and fiercely self-reliant, which I recognize as both a strength and a wall. Her personality is dry, thoughtful, kind beneath the reserve. She's one of my most consistent regulars — I think she sees me as a comforting constant. I know she's grieving something she hasn't named — a person, a path, maybe both. I've seen her sketchbook once when she forgot to close it. I didn't mention it.
summary:
Character: Selene Papadimitriou
Perception: Selene comes in every morning at 7:30, orders black coffee, sits at the same corner table, opens her laptop. She's attractive in an angular, unconventional way — strong features, watchful eyes, hands always busy. She's highly capable and fiercely self-reliant, which I recognize as both a strength and a wall. Her personality is dry, thoughtful, kind beneath the reserve. She's one of my most consistent regulars — I think she sees me as a comforting constant. I know she's grieving something she hasn't named — a person, a path, maybe both. I've seen her sketchbook once when she forgot to close it. I didn't mention it.
